skill.name: develop-userscripts
skill.description: Use when building, debugging, packaging, or publishing browser userscripts for Tampermonkey or ScriptCat, including GM APIs, metadata blocks, permission issues, @match/@grant/@connect setup, ScriptCat background or scheduled scripts, UserConfig blocks, or subscription workflows.
skill.summary: Build and debug Tampermonkey and ScriptCat browser userscripts with proper metadata and GM APIs.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files about userscript development patterns. All static analyzer findings are false positives: backtick detection represents markdown code markers for API names (GM_xmlhttpRequest, GM_getValue), URL detections are legitimate documentation links, and heuristic combination warnings arise from documenting cross-origin APIs which is expected for userscript documentation. No executable code or malicious patterns present.

content.value_statement: Building browser userscripts requires understanding metadata blocks, GM APIs, and runtime-specific differences between Tampermonkey and ScriptCat. This skill provides structured guidance for creating, debugging, and publishing userscripts with proper permissions and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Guide runtime selection between portable foreground scripts, ScriptCat background scripts, and cron scripts
Explain proper metadata block structure with @match, @grant, @connect, and @run-at directives
Document GM storage APIs including GM_getValue, GM_setValue, and async behavior differences
Cover cross-origin request handling with GM_xmlhttpRequest and @connect permissions
Provide debugging guidance for foreground console versus background script environments
limitations: Does not cover full browser extension development for Manifest V2 or V3 extensions
Does not provide executable code templates or generated userscript files
Does not cover general browser automation outside userscript managers

use_cases.title: Creating a new page modification script
use_cases.description: Developer wants to add custom UI to a web app. Uses this skill to understand @match patterns for page targeting, minimal @grant declarations for DOM access, and injection timing with @run-at.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers who want to customize websites they use frequently

use_cases.title: Building a cross-origin data fetcher
use_cases.description: Developer needs to call APIs from multiple domains. Uses this skill to properly configure GM_xmlhttpRequest with explicit @connect directives for each domain and understand cookie handling differences.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building scripts that aggregate data from multiple sources

use_cases.title: Creating a scheduled background task
use_cases.description: Developer wants a script that runs periodically in ScriptCat. Uses this skill to understand @crontab syntax, async promise completion requirements, and CATRetryError for retry logic.
use_cases.target_user: Users who need automated recurring tasks like price monitoring or notification checks

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic userscript
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Tampermonkey userscript that [describe what you want the script to do]. Include proper @match, @grant, and @connect directives.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - starting a new userscript project

prompt_templates.title: Debug permission issues
prompt_templates.prompt: My userscript is failing with [error message]. It uses [list GM APIs]. How do I fix the @grant and @connect setup?

prompt_templates.title: Convert to ScriptCat background script
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Tampermonkey script that [describe current behavior]. I need to run it as a ScriptCat background script because [reason]. What changes are needed for @background and async handling?
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - moving to ScriptCat background runtime

prompt_templates.title: Create a subscription package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ScriptCat subscription bundle that installs multiple related scripts. Include proper ==UserSubscribe== structure, @connect inheritance, and HTTPS requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - packaging multiple scripts for distribution

output_examples.input: I need a script that modifies the GitHub interface to show file sizes
output_examples.output: A userscript with @match for github.com, @grant for GM_addStyle and GM_xmlhttpRequest, @connect for api.github.com, and code that injects a size column into the file listing table.

output_examples.input: My ScriptCat background script is stopping too early
output_examples.output: Guidance to return a Promise from the script, await GM_xmlhttpRequest completion, and use CATRetryError instead of premature rejection for retry scenarios.

best_practices: Start with metadata, not implementation. Declare @match, @grant, @connect, and @run-at before writing any script logic.
Prefer portable patterns for ordinary page scripts. Only use ScriptCat-only features when the task genuinely needs background execution, cron, config blocks, or subscription packaging.
Declare the smallest permission surface that fits the task. Only @grant APIs that the script actually uses to keep permissions readable.
anti_patterns: Using @include instead of @match for ordinary site targeting when @match would suffice.
Returning from a ScriptCat background script before async GM work is finished, causing race conditions.
Mixing ==UserScript== and ==UserSubscribe== packaging concepts or placing ==UserConfig== in the wrong location.

faq.question: What is the difference between @grant and @connect?
faq.answer: @grant declares which GM APIs the script can use. @connect declares which external hosts the script can make requests to via GM_xmlhttpRequest or GM_cookie.

faq.question: When should I use ScriptCat @background instead of a normal userscript?
faq.answer: Use @background when you need persistent execution, state that survives page navigation, or work that continues after browser restart. Background scripts cannot access the page DOM.

faq.question: Why is my GM_getValue returning undefined after setting a value in ScriptCat?
faq.answer: In ScriptCat, GM_setValue(name, undefined) deletes the key instead of storing undefined. Also, ScriptCat storage operations are asynchronous, so ensure you await completion if the page may close immediately.

faq.question: How do I debug a ScriptCat background script?
faq.answer: Start with ScriptCat run log for basic debugging. For real-environment debugging, open the extension background.html page from extension settings. Note that value sync and menu commands do not work in the editor debugger.

faq.question: What is the difference between Tampermonkey and ScriptCat subscriptions?
faq.answer: Tampermonkey uses individual script @updateURL for updates. ScriptCat subscriptions use ==UserSubscribe== to bundle multiple scripts, with silent updates and subscription-level @connect that overrides child script permissions.

faq.question: How do I add user-editable settings to my ScriptCat script?
faq.answer: Add a ==UserConfig== block after ==UserScript== using YAML syntax. Config values are exposed through storage keys shaped like group.key, which you read with GM_getValue.
